ldr_strategic_goals.title: Reach $10M ARR by End of 2026
ldr_strategic_goals.description: Scale annual recurring revenue to $10M through a combination of net new business growth and expansion of existing accounts to larger tiers
ldr_strategic_goals.success_criteria: ARR reaches $10M or above as reported in the December 2026 billing cycle

ldr_strategic_goals.title: Achieve SOC 2 Type II Certification
ldr_strategic_goals.description: Complete a full SOC 2 Type II audit covering the Security, Availability, and Confidentiality trust service criteria to unlock enterprise and regulated-industry customers
ldr_strategic_goals.success_criteria: Receive SOC 2 Type II report from auditor covering a minimum 6-month observation period

ldr_strategic_goals.title: Launch Agent Platform to General Availability
ldr_strategic_goals.description: Move the Agent Data Layer and bot marketplace from early access to GA with a documented SLA, self-serve onboarding, and at least 50 active team deployments
ldr_strategic_goals.success_criteria: 50 active team deployments with >80% weekly retention at 60 days post-activation

ldr_okrs.objective: Accelerate new logo acquisition in Q2

key_results.title: Reduce sales cycle from 45 to 32 days

ldr_okrs.blockers: Enterprise deals stalled on SOC 2 requirements - accelerating compliance work may unblock 3-4 deals

key_results.title: Complete evidence collection for all 50 controls
Complete penetration test and remediate P0/P1 findings

ldr_okrs.blockers: 4 critical findings require engineering time that is currently allocated to GA launch. Need leadership decision on prioritization.

ldr_okrs.objective: Ship agent platform GA with self-serve activation

key_results.title: Publish self-serve activation docs and complete onboarding redesign

ldr_exec_briefings.title: Weekly Executive Summary W19 2026

key_signals.signal: 22 new logos closed in Q2 to date vs 40 target - pace tracking 55% of goal at mid-quarter
Agent platform at 34 active team deployments (68% of Q2 target). Retention holding at 83%.
SOC 2 evidence collection at 62% complete. 4 critical findings unresolved - engineering bandwidth conflict.
Order fulfillment SLA at 97.2% this week - slightly below 98% target. One carrier exception spike (FedEx, +1.8%).

action_items.action: Prioritization decision needed: SOC 2 critical findings vs GA launch sprint allocation
Review FedEx exception rate trend - consider carrier swap for overnight service level

ldr_exec_briefings.summary: Q2 sales is tracking behind pace at mid-quarter, with enterprise deals blocked by SOC 2 status. The agent platform is performing well with retention above target. A resource conflict between the SOC 2 remediation effort and the GA launch sprint requires an executive decision this week. Operations is within tolerance but watching carrier performance.

ldr_decision_log.title: Prioritize SOC 2 GA Over Feature Work in Q2
ldr_decision_log.decision: Allocate 40% of engineering capacity in Q2 to SOC 2 remediation and evidence collection, accepting a 2-week delay to the agent platform GA launch
ldr_decision_log.context: SOC 2 Type II is blocking 4 enterprise deals worth ~$800K ARR. Current completion pace would miss the Q3 audit window without additional engineering focus.
ldr_decision_log.alternatives_considered: Option A: Maintain current pace and push SOC 2 to Q4 audit window. Option B: Hire a contractor to handle SOC 2 evidence collection. Option C: Reduce feature scope and reallocate 40% of engineering.
ldr_decision_log.rationale: Option C was chosen because the ROI of unblocking enterprise deals ($800K ARR) outweighs the 2-week GA delay. Contractor option rejected due to context-transfer time cost. Option A risks missing the annual SOC 2 target entirely.
ldr_decision_log.expected_outcome: SOC 2 audit readiness by September 30, agent platform GA by August 31, 3-4 enterprise deals unblocked in Q2-Q3

ldr_decision_log.title: Sunset Legacy Console Port 3001
ldr_decision_log.decision: Decommission the standalone console application on port 3001 and complete the migration to the unified frontend on port 3000 by end of Q1 2026
ldr_decision_log.context: Two separate frontend applications (landing/docs on 3000, workspace console on 3001) created inconsistent UX, duplicated infrastructure, and slowed feature development across both surfaces.
ldr_decision_log.alternatives_considered: Option A: Keep both frontends and share a component library. Option B: Migrate all surfaces to a unified frontend. Option C: Use a micro-frontend architecture with a shared shell.
ldr_decision_log.rationale: Option B delivers the best developer experience and UX consistency. The migration is estimated at 6 weeks of effort with a payoff of reduced operational complexity and a single deployment surface.
ldr_decision_log.expected_outcome: Single frontend at port 3000 serving landing, docs, and workspace console. Zero customer-visible downtime during migration.
ldr_decision_log.actual_outcome: Completed December 2024. Zero downtime. Developer velocity on cross-surface features improved by an estimated 30%.

ldr_decision_log.title: Adopt Kolumn as Sole Schema Management Tool
ldr_decision_log.decision: Mandate Kolumn HCL for all database schema changes. Direct SQL DDL is banned across all repositories effective immediately.
ldr_decision_log.context: Multiple schema drift incidents caused by ad-hoc SQL run outside the migration system. Three production incidents in Q4 2025 were traced to undocumented schema changes.
ldr_decision_log.alternatives_considered: Option A: Enforce SQL migration files with a standard migration tool (Flyway, Goose). Option B: Mandate Kolumn HCL for all schema changes. Option C: Continue ad-hoc with better documentation.
ldr_decision_log.rationale: Kolumn provides state tracking, rollback capability, audit trails, and is already the bundled IaC tool for SchemaBounce customers - using it internally strengthens the product by making us eat our own cooking.
ldr_decision_log.expected_outcome: Zero schema drift incidents caused by undocumented SQL changes. All schema state tracked in version control.
ldr_decision_log.actual_outcome: No schema drift incidents since mandate. Two instances where engineers attempted direct SQL and were caught by pre-commit hooks.
